summary:
Phenytoin commonly causes gingival hyperplasia; while several side effects exist, dental tissue growth abnormalities occur most frequently based on extensive research documentation. This conclusion maintains consistency across multiple sources emphasizing its prominence above others like cardiovascular concerns or skin conditions when assessing overall frequency for patients taking phenytoin regularly. To summarize concisely:

Gingival hyperplasia is one of the most common side effects due to pheno-toin use, occurring far more often than many other possible complications such as heart-related problems or rashes according to numerous studies highlighting their consistent reporting patterns indicating significant incidence rates linked specifically back to oral soft tissues enlargement noted clinically during treatment
